data.description:
## Summary
Three tests pass locally (uncontended) but fail intermittently under loaded CI runners, blocking the Gerrit Verified gate and forcing repeated rechecks (observed 4+ times in one session). Each has a specific, changeable root cause — not generic 'flakiness'.

## Root causes (confirmed by reading + local repro)
1. **tests/scripts/graph/test_graph_perf.py::test_graph_build_scales_linearly** — asserts algorithmic complexity via WALL-CLOCK ratio t(2N)/t(N) < 3.0. The ratio only cancels UNIFORM load; a transient pause during one of the two sequential measurements skews it (observed 3.26, 4.44). REDUNDANT: the same file's deterministic call-count tests (test_build_dep_graph_single_batch_scan asserts exactly 1 reduce_all_tickets call; test_find_direct_blockers_no_per_ticket_scan / test_compute_dep_graph_children_use_preloaded_state assert 0 rescans) already lock the O(n) invariant. (test_hierarchy_enforcement_benchmark_relative is the same wall-clock-ratio class, latent.)
2. **tests/unit/grounding/test_harness.py::test_worker_hard_crash_maps_to_parse_error_not_host_crash** — in src/rebar/grounding/harness.py run_in_worker, the branch 'if payload is None and proc.is_alive(): return timeout' (line ~341) is racy: on SIGABRT the pipe closes (poll returns EOF, payload=None) but the just-killed child may not be reaped yet, so is_alive() transiently returns True -> misclassified 'timeout' instead of falling through to exitcode<0 -> 'parse_error' (line ~359). Correct discriminator is whether poll(call_timeout) TIMED OUT (still working) vs returned readable/EOF (exited/crashed). Violates the docstring contract (lines 287-288) + the test.
3. **tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py::test_run_acli_429_retries_with_rate_limit_backoff** — patches acli_subprocess.time.sleep (the GLOBAL time module) and asserts delays == [1.0]. acli's own two sleep sites produce 2s/4s/Retry-After values, so the observed extra 0.001..0.05 series comes from OTHER code (a reap/poll loop) via the over-broad global patch; count is nondeterministic under load. Fix scopes the assertion to the retry backoff, not all process sleeps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The three tests are deterministic — assert invariants/behavior, not wall-clock or global sleep counts — and cannot fail from runner contention.
- [ ] harness.run_in_worker classifies a signal-killed worker as parse_error regardless of reap timing; a RED test reproduces the misclassification deterministically (inject the is_alive race) and passes after the fix; mutation (revert) returns it to RED.
- [ ] The O(n) build invariant remains covered by the deterministic call-count tests after the wall-clock tests are removed/demoted; genuine perf/benchmark checks do not gate the merge.
- [ ] The acli 429 test asserts exactly the Retry-After backoff behavior, robust to unrelated process-reap sleeps.
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
data.title: CI flakes: three merge-gating tests fail under loaded runners (wall-clock/timing/over-broad mock), forcing rechecks
